title: Difference between SQS Visibility Timeout and Delay Queue

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Navigate to the Services menu on the top, select Simple Queue Service under Application Integration.
Make sure you are in the N. Virginia region.
Click on the Create queue button.
Task 2: Create a SQS Queue
Details : Type : Select Standard Name : Enter lab-sqs
Details :
Type : Select Standard
Name : Enter lab-sqs
Task 2: Create a SQS Queue
Configuration : Visibility timeout : Enter 1 Minute Leave everything as default.
Configuration :
Visibility timeout : Enter 1 Minute
Leave everything as default.
Task 2: Create a SQS Queue
Click on the Create queue button.
Task 2: Create a SQS Queue
Now copy the Queue URL under Details and place it in the text editor, we will be using this while creating the lambda function.

Click on the link https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/home?region=us-east-1#/functions to open the Lambda console in a new tab in the browser. It is important to have both SQS and Lambda functions opened in two tabs.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) region.

Click on the Create Function Choose Author From Scratch. Function name: Enter my_sqs_Lambda Runtime: Select Python 3.8 Permissions: Click on the Change default execution role and choose Use an existing role Existing role: Select task165_role_<RANDOM_NUMBER> from the dropdown list. Click on Create function button.
Click on the Create Function
Choose Author From Scratch.
Function name: Enter my_sqs_Lambda
Runtime: Select Python 3.8
Permissions: Click on the Change default execution role and choose Use an existing role
Existing role: Select task165_role_<RANDOM_NUMBER> from the dropdown list.
Click on Create function button.
Task 3: Create a lambda function
Code section, Replace the existing code with the below
Note: Please paste the SQS Queue URL which you copied and placed in the text editor.
Task 3: Create a lambda function
Save the function by clicking on Deploy in the top right corner.
Now click on the Test button, Event name: Enter LambdaTest and click on the Save button.

tasks.title: Task 4: Send a message with Visibility Timeout

steps.text: Go to the SQS Queue browser tab.
Click on the Send and receive messages button on the top right side.
Under Send message: Message body: Enter Test Visibility timeout
Under Send message:
Message body: Enter Test Visibility timeout
Now click on the Send message button.
You will be able to see a similar message.
Task 4: Send a message with Visibility Timeout
Now go to the Lambda function Browser tab.
Click on the Test button of your function and check the lambda function execution output.
You will be able to see the message that you have sent in the Queue, as shown below.
Task 4: Send a message with Visibility Timeout
Now again click on the Test button and check the lambda output.
You won't be able to see the message, this is because once the message is received by the lambda function (Consumer), the visibility timeout starts, which you have set to 1 Minute.
Task 4: Send a message with Visibility Timeout
For 1 Minute the message will not be available in the queue to be received by the consumer. This is what visibility timeout is set.
Try to Test the lambda function after 1 Minute and you will be able to receive the message again.
Task 4: Send a message with Visibility Timeout
Navigate to the SQS browser tab.
Click on the queue name on the top left of the page.
Task 5: Purge the Queue
Now click on the Purge button.
Enter purge in the confirm box and click on the Purge button.
Purge will delete all the messages present in the queue.

tasks.title: Task 6: Send a message with Delivery Delay

steps.text: Click on the Send and receive messages button on the top right side.
Under Send message: Message body: Enter Test delivery delay Delivery Delay: Enter 30 Seconds
Under Send message:
Message body: Enter Test delivery delay
Delivery Delay: Enter 30 Seconds
Task 6: Send a message with Delivery Delay
Now click on the Send message button.
You will be able to see a similar message.
Task 6: Send a message with Delivery Delay
Now go to the Lambda function Browser tab.
Click on the Test button of your function and check the lambda function execution output.
Task 6: Send a message with Delivery Delay
The message is not available, because we have added a delay to deliver the message to the consumer (Lambda function)
Now wait for 30 seconds and then again click on the Test button of your function and check the lambda function execution output.
Task 6: Send a message with Delivery Delay
Navigate to the SQS browser tab.
Click on the queue name on the top left of the page.
Task 7: Purge the Queue
Now click on the Purge button.
Enter purge in the confirm box and click on the Purge button.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 8 : Validation of the Lab
Navigate to the Services menu on the top and select Simple Queue Service under Application Integration.
Make sure you are in the N. Virginia region.
In the left top side click on the Menu icon and then click on Queues.
Select your SQS Queue lab-sqs and click on the Delete button.
Task 9: Delete AWS Resources
Enter delete in the confirm box and click on the Delete button.
Navigate to Lambda by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on Lambda in the Compute section.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) region.
Select the lambda function my_sqs_Lambda and click Actions and click the Delete button.
Task 9: Delete AWS Resources
Click the Delete button to confirm the deletion.
You have successfully created a SQS queue.
You have successfully created a Lambda function.
You have successfully sent a message with Visibility Timeout.
You have successfully purged the Queue.
You have successfully sent a message with Delivery Delay.
You have successfully purged the Queue.
Sign out of the AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from your lab’s dashboard.
